project.name: box-agent
project.description: A general-purpose AI agent framework with a sandboxed Jupyter code-execution kernel, parallel sub-agents, MCP tool integration, the ACP protocol, and multi-provider LLM support (Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepSeek, SiliconFlow, and any compatible API), plus a standalone PyInstaller runtime and two-layer automatic context compression.

nodes.name: mcp_loader.py
nodes.summary: Loads and manages MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool servers over stdio/SSE/streamable-HTTP transports, wrapping remote tools as Tool instances with dynamic bearer auth and lazy loading.
nodes.languageNotes: Async MCP client wiring with httpx auth flow and multiple transport connectors.

nodes.name: MCPServerConnection
nodes.summary: Manages a single MCP server connection lifecycle, selecting stdio/SSE/streamable-HTTP transport and exposing connect/disconnect with configurable timeouts.

nodes.name: MCPTool
nodes.summary: Adapter that wraps a remote MCP tool as a local Tool, forwarding execute() calls to the MCP server connection.

nodes.name: DynamicBearerAuth
nodes.summary: httpx auth flow that re-resolves the bearer token per request so MCP HTTP connections always use the current credential.

nodes.name: load_mcp_tools_async
nodes.summary: Reads the MCP config, connects to each enabled (non-lazy) server, and returns the aggregated list of wrapped MCPTool instances.

nodes.name: ensure_lazy_mcp_loaded
nodes.summary: Lazily connects pending MCP servers on demand (e.g., triggered by a query) and registers their tools.

nodes.name: _resolve_mcp_config_path
nodes.summary: Resolves the active mcp.json path across dev, user, and package configuration locations.

nodes.name: set_mcp_timeout_config
nodes.summary: Sets module-level MCP connect/execute/SSE-read timeout configuration shared by all connections.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Utils package barrel that re-exports terminal display helpers for convenient importing.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel re-exporting terminal_utils helpers.

nodes.name: terminal_utils.py
nodes.summary: Terminal display width helpers handling CJK/wide characters for calculating, truncating, and padding text to a target column width.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses unicodedata east-asian-width to account for wide glyphs in column math.

nodes.name: calculate_display_width
nodes.summary: Computes the rendered terminal column width of a string, counting CJK/wide characters as two columns.

nodes.name: truncate_with_ellipsis
nodes.summary: Truncates text to a maximum display width, appending an ellipsis while respecting wide-character boundaries.

nodes.name: pad_to_width
nodes.summary: Pads text to a target display width with a configurable alignment and fill character, accounting for wide glyphs.

nodes.name: 02_simple_agent.py
nodes.summary: Example demonstrating a basic agent that creates files and runs bash tasks using the built-in tools.

nodes.name: demo_file_creation
nodes.summary: Demonstrates running an agent that creates files in the workspace with write/edit tools.

nodes.name: demo_bash_task
nodes.summary: Demonstrates running an agent that executes a shell task via the bash tool.

nodes.name: 04_full_agent.py
nodes.summary: Example showing a fully configured agent with MCP tools and an interactive mode walkthrough.

nodes.name: demo_full_agent
nodes.summary: Builds and runs a fully featured agent with config, tools, and MCP loading.

nodes.name: demo_interactive_mode
nodes.summary: Demonstrates driving the agent in an interactive multi-turn conversation loop.

nodes.name: 05_provider_selection.py
nodes.summary: Example illustrating how to select and compare Anthropic, OpenAI-compatible, and default LLM providers.

nodes.name: demo_provider_comparison
nodes.summary: Compares responses across multiple configured LLM providers side by side.

nodes.name: 06_tool_schema_demo.py
nodes.summary: Example defining several custom Tool subclasses and demonstrating their Anthropic/OpenAI schema generation.

nodes.name: WeatherTool
nodes.summary: Example Tool subclass exposing a weather lookup with parameter schema and execute implementation.

nodes.name: SearchTool
nodes.summary: Example Tool subclass demonstrating a search tool schema and execute method.

nodes.name: CalculatorTool
nodes.summary: Example Tool subclass providing a calculator with a parameter schema and execute implementation.

nodes.name: TranslateTool
nodes.summary: Example Tool subclass exposing a translation tool with schema and execute method.

nodes.name: demo_tool_schemas
nodes.summary: Prints the generated Anthropic and OpenAI schemas for the example tools.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package root that re-exports the public API (Agent, event types, hooks, LLM client) so consumers can import everything from box_agent directly.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using __init__.py re-exports.

nodes.name: agent.py
nodes.summary: Public Agent wrapper API that drives the core async event loop, exposing run_events() for the raw event stream and run() for terminal rendering with cancellation and injection support.
nodes.languageNotes: AsyncGenerator-based event consumption wrapping run_agent_loop.

nodes.name: Agent
nodes.summary: Primary public class wrapping the agent loop: manages messages, tools, logging, cancellation, and renders events to the terminal.

nodes.name: _format_size
nodes.summary: Formats a byte count into a human-readable size string for artifact rendering.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: Authentication helpers that resolve bearer tokens from explicit values, token files, or environment variables and attach Authorization headers to trusted request URLs.

nodes.name: read_auth_token_file
nodes.summary: Reads and parses a bearer token from a JSON or plain-text auth file, tolerating missing files and malformed content.

nodes.name: resolve_auth_token
nodes.summary: Resolves a bearer token by precedence: explicit value, then auth file, then environment variables.

nodes.name: bearer_auth_headers
nodes.summary: Builds an Authorization header dict for a bearer token, skipping URLs not on the trusted host allowlist.

nodes.name: should_attach_auth_header
nodes.summary: Determines whether to attach an auth header to a URL based on a trusted-host suffix check.

nodes.name: cli.py
nodes.summary: Interactive command-line entry point providing the setup wizard, doctor/config/log subcommands, browser/node installers, and the main agent REPL loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Large CLI module using prompt_toolkit and argparse subparsers.

nodes.name: run_setup_wizard
nodes.summary: Interactive first-run wizard that prompts for provider, API base, model, and key, then writes the config YAML.

nodes.name: show_log_directory
nodes.summary: Lists recent log files in the log directory and optionally opens it in the OS file manager.

nodes.name: read_log_file
nodes.summary: Reads and prints a named log file from the log directory, handling missing-file cases.

nodes.name: print_session_info
nodes.summary: Renders a boxed session info banner showing workspace, model, tool counts, and skills at REPL startup.

nodes.name: print_stats
nodes.summary: Prints session statistics including duration, message counts, and aggregated token usage.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Defines the argparse parser and subcommands (log, setup, config, doctor, install-browser, install-node) for the CLI.

nodes.name: cmd_config
nodes.summary: Implements the config subcommand: locates and prints the active config, or opens it in an editor.

nodes.name: cmd_doctor
nodes.summary: Implements the doctor subcommand: checks environment, config, API connectivity, and browser runtime status.

nodes.name: _doctor_check_browser
nodes.summary: Checks whether the Playwright Chromium runtime is installed at the configured browsers path.

nodes.name: cmd_install_browser
nodes.summary: Installs the Playwright Chromium browser into the shared cache and enables the playwright MCP server.

nodes.name: cmd_install_node
nodes.summary: Installs a bundled Node.js runtime used by Node-based skills and MCP servers.

nodes.name: _enable_playwright_in_mcp
nodes.summary: Flips the playwright MCP server's disabled flag to false in the user's mcp.json config.

nodes.name: _quiet_cleanup
nodes.summary: Suppresses noisy shutdown errors when tearing down async resources at CLI exit.

nodes.name: run_agent
nodes.summary: Core CLI driver that builds the Agent with tools/MCP/skills/sandbox, runs the interactive REPL or non-interactive task, handles slash commands, permissions, and memory review.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses arguments, dispatches subcommands, and launches the agent REPL.

nodes.name: cli_memory_proposal.py
nodes.summary: CLI-side negotiator that prompts the user via termios to pin, skip, or reject memory promotion candidates, rendering a diff of proposed core-memory changes.

nodes.name: CLIMemoryProposalNegotiator
nodes.summary: Drives the terminal prompt flow for memory promotion proposals, supporting both plan-based and legacy candidate negotiation.

nodes.name: _render_diff
nodes.summary: Renders a colorized, line-limited diff between old and new memory content for the proposal prompt.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic configuration models and loader for the agent framework, covering LLM, tools, memory, hooks, and permissions, with layered config-file resolution.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic BaseModel hierarchy with layered dev/installed/package config resolution.

nodes.name: Config
nodes.summary: Top-level configuration aggregate with loader methods that resolve config files across dev, user, and package directories and bootstrap user config.

nodes.name: LLMConfig
nodes.summary: LLM provider configuration model with a computed context_token_limit derived from context window and max output tokens.

nodes.name: AgentConfig
nodes.summary: Agent behavior configuration including memory extraction, summarization thresholds, and workspace settings.

nodes.name: ToolsConfig
nodes.summary: Tool-enablement and filesystem/memory permission configuration for the agent's built-in tools.

nodes.name: events.py
nodes.summary: Defines the full set of structured AgentEvent dataclasses (step, thinking, content, tool-call, artifact, memory, permission, done) yielded by the core agent loop.
nodes.languageNotes: 24 dataclass event types forming the agent loop's event protocol.

nodes.name: ArtifactEvent
nodes.summary: Event carrying detected artifact metadata (kind, filename, paths, mime, size, sha256) emitted when a tool produces an output file.

nodes.name: PermissionRequestEvent
nodes.summary: Event representing an in-band permission request for out-of-workspace access, carrying scope and option metadata.

nodes.name: MemoryProposalEvent
nodes.summary: Event proposing CONTEXT-to-core memory promotion candidates, consumed by CLI/ACP negotiators before the terminal DoneEvent.

nodes.name: hooks.py
nodes.summary: Lifecycle hooks system defining BaseHook callbacks and a HookManager that fires agent/step/tool/done/error events to registered hook classes.

nodes.name: BaseHook
nodes.summary: Base class defining the 8 overridable lifecycle hook points (agent start, step start/end, llm response, tool start/result, done, error).

nodes.name: HookManager
nodes.summary: Registry that holds hook instances and fires each lifecycle event to all registered hooks, supporting interceptors.

nodes.name: load_hooks
nodes.summary: Dynamically imports and instantiates hook classes from dotted class paths, ensuring the user hooks directory is on sys.path.

nodes.name: setup.py
nodes.summary: Tool registry bootstrap: initializes base/memory/bash/skill/MCP tools and assembles workspace-scoped tools (sandbox, file, todo, vision, image, sub-agent) according to config and permissions.

nodes.name: skill_loader.py
nodes.summary: Loads SKILL.md files with YAML frontmatter into Skill objects, discovers and caches skills across sources, and provides query-based filtering and metadata prompts via SkillLoader/SkillSelector.
nodes.languageNotes: Frontmatter parsed via regex + yaml.safe_load; mtime-signature based reload caching.

nodes.name: skill_tool.py
nodes.summary: GetSkillTool that surfaces a named skill's full prompt on demand, plus create_skill_tools factory that builds a SkillLoader over the configured skill sources.

nodes.name: sub_agent_tool.py
nodes.summary: SubAgentTool that runs a delegated task in an isolated message context via the agent loop, streaming child progress as SubAgentEvent through the EventEmittingTool queue.

nodes.name: todo_tool.py
nodes.summary: In-memory TodoStore plus TodoWriteTool/TodoReadTool for tracking task lists during an agent run, with snapshot formatting for tool output.

nodes.name: vision_review_tool.py
nodes.summary: VisionReviewTool that sends a workspace image to a vision-capable model and returns a normalized review report, resolving readable/writable paths under safety constraints.

nodes.name: 01_basic_tools.py
nodes.summary: Runnable example demonstrating the Write, Read, Edit, and Bash tools directly without a full agent loop.

nodes.name: initialize_base_tools
nodes.summary: Assembles the base tool set (memory, bash output/kill, skills) and kicks off async MCP tool loading.

nodes.name: merge_mcp_tools
nodes.summary: Merges loaded MCP tools into the base tool list, replacing any base tool whose name collides.

nodes.name: add_workspace_tools
nodes.summary: Builds workspace-scoped tools (sandbox bash, file Read/Write/Edit, todo, vision, image, sub-agent) wiring permission engine, sandbox mode, and skill runtime context.

nodes.name: Skill
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a loaded skill (name, description, content, keywords, metadata) with prompt and metadata serialization.

nodes.name: SkillLoader
nodes.summary: Discovers, parses, and caches SKILL.md files across sources with mtime-based reload, query filtering, and metadata prompt generation.

nodes.name: SkillSelector
nodes.summary: Maintains a cumulative query across turns and binds the most relevant skills' metadata prompt via the loader's filter.

nodes.name: GetSkillTool
nodes.summary: Tool that returns the full prompt body for a named skill, reloading the loader on demand and listing available skills on miss.

nodes.name: create_skill_tools
nodes.summary: Factory that builds a SkillLoader over the given sources, discovers skills, and returns the GetSkillTool.

nodes.name: SubAgentTool
nodes.summary: EventEmittingTool that runs a delegated task in an isolated agent loop (excluding itself to prevent recursion) and streams child progress as SubAgentEvents.

nodes.name: TodoStore
nodes.summary: In-memory todo store with id allocation and create/update/delete/get/list operations.

nodes.name: TodoWriteTool
nodes.summary: Tool for creating and mutating todo items, emitting a formatted snapshot of the current list.

nodes.name: TodoReadTool
nodes.summary: Tool for reading and formatting the current todo list.

nodes.name: VisionReviewTool
nodes.summary: Tool that submits a workspace image to a vision model, builds provider-specific content blocks, and returns a normalized review report with safe path resolution.

nodes.name: demo_bash_tool
nodes.summary: Example coroutine demonstrating the BashTool by running shell commands and printing results.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Example entry point that runs the write/read/edit/bash tool demos in sequence.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the tools subsystem; re-exports the core tool classes and factory helpers so consumers can import them from a single namespace.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package __init__ acting as a re-export barrel.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract Tool base class (with to_schema/to_openai_schema/parallel_safe), the ToolResult Pydantic model, and EventEmittingTool for queue-based progress events.
nodes.languageNotes: Abstract base via NotImplementedError; ToolResult is a Pydantic model.

nodes.name: bash_tool.py
nodes.summary: Implements the BashTool plus background shell management (BackgroundShellManager/BackgroundShell) and the BashOutput/BashKill companion tools, wiring command execution through safety and scope-escape checks.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses asyncio.create_subprocess_exec and per-shell monitor tasks for background execution.

nodes.name: file_tools.py
nodes.summary: Provides the Read/Write/Edit file tools with workspace path validation, token-aware truncation, and model-context summarization for read previews.

nodes.name: image_generation_tool.py
nodes.summary: GenerateImageTool that calls OpenAI-compatible and Seedream image endpoints, normalizing sizes/prompts and decoding base64 or URL image payloads to files under the workspace.
nodes.languageNotes: Async httpx client with content-type sniffing and base64/data-URL decoding helpers.

nodes.name: memory_tool.py
nodes.summary: Exposes MemoryWriteTool, MemoryReadTool, and MemorySearchTool, delegating to the MemoryManager for the dual-file core/context memory store.

nodes.name: pptx_safety.py
nodes.summary: Heuristic detectors that flag attempts to bypass the PPTX self-check (direct DOM export, exporter mentions, self-check removal) used by the write and bash tools.

nodes.name: safety.py
nodes.summary: Safety layer with dangerous-command detection, workspace scope-escape checks, path validation, auto-backup, and rm-target extraction shared across bash and file tools.

nodes.name: ToolResult
nodes.summary: Pydantic result model returned by every tool, carrying success/content/error plus optional permission_request, raw_output, and model_context fields.

nodes.name: Tool
nodes.summary: Abstract base class defining the tool contract (name/description/parameters/execute) and Anthropic/OpenAI schema serialization plus a parallel_safe flag.

nodes.name: EventEmittingTool
nodes.summary: Tool subclass that adds an asyncio.Queue for emitting real-time progress events, reused by long-running tools like SubAgentTool.

nodes.name: BackgroundShell
nodes.summary: Represents a single background shell process, buffering output lines, tracking status, and supporting graceful termination.

nodes.name: BackgroundShellManager
nodes.summary: Class-level registry of background shells that spawns monitor tasks to stream process output and coordinates termination and cleanup.

nodes.name: BashTool
nodes.summary: Primary shell execution tool that runs commands through dangerous-command and scope-escape checks, supports background mode, and resolves a login shell and bundled runtime environment.

nodes.name: BashOutputTool
nodes.summary: Tool for polling new output from a running background shell by bash_id.

nodes.name: BashKillTool
nodes.summary: Tool for terminating a running background shell and reporting its final output.

nodes.name: truncate_text_by_tokens
nodes.summary: Truncates text to a token budget using tiktoken, keeping a head/tail window and snapping to line boundaries.

nodes.name: build_read_file_model_context
nodes.summary: Builds a compact model-facing context for a read file, summarizing JSON or capping preview lines for generated artifacts.

nodes.name: ReadTool
nodes.summary: Reads a file with line numbering, workspace path validation, token-aware truncation, and a separate compact model_context.

nodes.name: WriteTool
nodes.summary: Writes file content after permission and workspace checks, auto-backup, and PPTX self-check bypass detection.

nodes.name: EditTool
nodes.summary: Performs string-replacement edits on a file with permission/workspace validation, backup, and PPTX bypass detection.

nodes.name: GenerateImageTool
nodes.summary: Generates images via OpenAI-compatible/Seedream endpoints, resolving output paths, checking write permission, requesting the image, and persisting the decoded bytes.

nodes.name: MemoryWriteTool
nodes.summary: Writes/appends entries to core (MEMORY.md) or context (CONTEXT.md) via the MemoryManager, optionally with LLM-assisted update.

nodes.name: MemoryReadTool
nodes.summary: Reads back the core and/or context memory files for inspection.

nodes.name: MemorySearchTool
nodes.summary: Searches the context memory store and returns ranked matching entries.

nodes.name: detect_pptx_self_check_bypass
nodes.summary: Top-level detector combining the heuristic sub-checks to flag content that attempts to skip the PPTX exporter self-check.

nodes.name: detect_dangerous_command
nodes.summary: Scans a shell command against known dangerous patterns (rm, sudo, kill, etc.) and returns the matched reason.

nodes.name: detect_scope_escape
nodes.summary: Detects commands that reach outside the workspace by extracting and validating path tokens against the workspace and dev allowlist.

nodes.name: validate_path_in_workspace
nodes.summary: Resolves a path and confirms it stays within the workspace directory.

nodes.name: backup_file
nodes.summary: Copies an existing file into a timestamped trash directory before destructive modification.

nodes.name: extract_rm_targets
nodes.summary: Parses an rm command with shlex to resolve the concrete file targets being deleted.

nodes.name: intent_classifier.py
nodes.summary: Auto-classifies an ACP session's mode from the first user prompt using a single tool-free LLM call with regex fast-paths and a whitelist-validated, timeout-guarded result.

nodes.name: classify_session_mode
nodes.summary: Determines the session mode for a prompt via regex deliverable detection then a fallback LLM classification call, with an 8s timeout and whitelist normalization; returns None on failure.

nodes.name: core.py
nodes.summary: Execution core of the agent framework; run_agent_loop is the single source of truth for the agent loop, yielding structured events, doing two-layer context compression, cancellation, permission negotiation, and artifact detection.
nodes.languageNotes: run_agent_loop is an AsyncGenerator yielding AgentEvent objects; no print/input I/O so consumers (CLI, ACP) drive presentation.

nodes.name: run_agent_loop
nodes.summary: The central async generator agent loop: streams LLM output, executes tools (sequential and parallel), drains progress queues, handles cancellation, permission negotiation, memory recall/extraction, summarization, and artifact emission as structured events.

nodes.name: _make_artifact
nodes.summary: Builds a unified ArtifactEvent from a produced file, computing rel path, mime type, kind, size, sha256 hash, and file URI under the workspace output directory.

nodes.name: _maybe_summarize
nodes.summary: Layer-2 context compression: when estimated tokens exceed the limit, replaces older messages with an LLM-generated summary while preserving recent turns and summary markers.

nodes.name: _micro_compact
nodes.summary: Layer-1 zero-cost context compression run every step: replaces tool results older than the last few with short placeholders, shrinking the keep window when recent results blow the token budget.

nodes.name: _create_summary
nodes.summary: Generates an LLM summary of a batch of older conversation messages for context compression, flattening tool calls and results into a compact prompt.

nodes.name: _auto_match_memory_for_latest_prompt
nodes.summary: Finds the latest user prompt and auto-matches relevant CONTEXT.md memory entries, injecting them as a synthetic tool result into the message history.

nodes.name: _detect_artifacts
nodes.summary: Regex-based artifact detection: scans tool output for [filename.ext] references that resolve under the workspace output directory and builds ArtifactEvents for each.

nodes.name: _detect_new_files
nodes.summary: Workspace-diff artifact detection: compares pre/post file snapshots to emit ArtifactEvents for files newly created by any tool, skipping already-emitted ones.

nodes.name: _summarize_tool_argument_for_model
nodes.summary: Compacts a large tool-call argument (e.g. file content) into a short summary for the model-facing history, preserving head and tail lines based on file type.

nodes.name: _sanitize_dangling_tool_calls
nodes.summary: Repairs message history by inserting placeholder tool results for tool calls that lack matching responses, preventing API rejection of dangling tool_use blocks.

nodes.name: _cleanup_incomplete_messages
nodes.summary: Trims trailing incomplete or empty messages from history so the conversation ends in a state the LLM API accepts.

nodes.name: _estimate_tokens
nodes.summary: Estimates token count of a message list using tiktoken, falling back to a character-based heuristic when the encoder is unavailable.

nodes.name: _tool_message_content_for_model
nodes.summary: Produces the model-facing form of a tool result, applying visible-content compaction and stripping inline plot data to reduce token usage.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the LLM layer, re-exporting the LLMClient wrapper and the provider-specific Anthropic/OpenAI clients plus the lightweight one-shot helper.

nodes.name: anthropic_client.py
nodes.summary: Anthropic-protocol LLM client handling message/tool conversion, request preparation, streaming and non-streaming generation, and native thinking budget support.
nodes.languageNotes: Sends thinking={"type": "enabled", "budget_tokens": 8000} when thinking_enabled; api_base used as-is for any Anthropic-compatible endpoint.

nodes.name: AnthropicClient
nodes.summary: Anthropic API client implementing LLMClientBase: converts internal messages/tools to Anthropic format, issues requests with retry, and parses responses into the framework's LLMResponse/stream events.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Abstract base class defining the LLM client interface (generate, generate_stream, message conversion, auth headers) shared by the Anthropic and OpenAI implementations.

nodes.name: LLMClientBase
nodes.summary: Abstract interface for provider LLM clients, declaring generate/generate_stream and shared helpers for auth headers, request preparation, and message conversion.

nodes.name: debug_logging.py
nodes.summary: Structured LLM request/response debug logging with payload sanitization, sensitive-key redaction, request-id extraction, and a pluggable debug sink controlled by env vars.
nodes.languageNotes: Redacts sensitive keys and sanitizes URLs/headers before emitting; full payload logging gated by env var.

nodes.name: log_llm_request
nodes.summary: Emits a sanitized, optionally-summarized log record for an outgoing LLM request through the configured debug sink.

nodes.name: sanitize_for_logging
nodes.summary: Recursively redacts sensitive keys and truncates large values in a payload so it is safe to write to debug logs.

nodes.name: summarize_request_payload_for_logging
nodes.summary: Builds a compact summary of an LLM request payload (messages, tools, schemas) for logging when full-payload logging is disabled.

nodes.name: lightweight.py
nodes.summary: One-shot LLM prompt helper for title/summary/classification calls, wrapping LLMClient.generate with a timeout and returning a structured result or typed error.

nodes.name: run_lightweight_prompt
nodes.summary: Runs a single tool-free, history-free LLM prompt with an asyncio timeout, returning a LightweightResult or raising typed timeout/invalid-args errors.

nodes.name: LightweightPromptError
nodes.summary: Base exception for lightweight prompt failures, carrying a structured error code and message for ACP error envelopes.

nodes.name: llm_wrapper.py
nodes.summary: LLMClient facade that selects and delegates to the Anthropic or OpenAI provider client, adds retry callbacks, and applies think-tag splitting to streamed output.

nodes.name: LLMClient
nodes.summary: Public LLM facade wrapping a provider-specific client; exposes generate/generate_stream, manages a retry callback, and normalizes inline think-tags in streamed deltas.

nodes.name: openai_client.py
nodes.summary: OpenAI-protocol LLM client handling message/tool conversion, request preparation, and streaming/non-streaming generation against any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
nodes.languageNotes: api_base passed through unchanged; reasoning/thinking fields sent for providers that support them and silently ignored otherwise.

nodes.name: OpenAIClient
nodes.summary: OpenAI API client implementing LLMClientBase: converts internal messages/tools to OpenAI format, issues requests with retry, and parses streamed/full responses into framework types.

nodes.name: think_tag_splitter.py
nodes.summary: Utilities to detect and split inline <think> tags from model output, separating reasoning content from visible content in a stateful streaming-aware manner.

nodes.name: split_inline_think
nodes.summary: Splits a text chunk into thinking and content segments based on inline <think> tag boundaries, preserving state across streaming calls.

nodes.name: unwrap_think_tags
nodes.summary: Strips wrapping <think> tags from a complete text, returning the separated reasoning and visible content.

nodes.name: logger.py
nodes.summary: Per-run agent logger that captures full LLM requests/responses, tool results, and debug records to timestamped log files, preserving originals lost to context compression.

nodes.name: AgentLogger
nodes.summary: Logger that starts a new run file per session and records requests, responses, tool results, and structured LLM debug records to disk for diagnostics.

nodes.name: retry.py
nodes.summary: Async retry infrastructure providing the @async_retry decorator with exponential backoff, retryable-error detection, and typed exhaustion/stream-interruption exceptions.

nodes.name: async_retry
nodes.summary: Decorator that retries an async callable with exponential backoff and jitter, raising RetryExhaustedError after the configured attempts are used up.

nodes.name: RetryConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for retry behavior (max attempts, base delay, backoff factor, max delay) with delay calculation including jitter.

nodes.name: StreamInterrupted
nodes.summary: Exception capturing a mid-stream interruption with partial accumulated content so callers can decide whether to resume or fail.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Schema package barrel re-exporting the core data models (Message, ToolCall, LLMResponse, TokenUsage, etc.) from schema.py.

nodes.name: schema.py
nodes.summary: Core data models for the LLM layer: provider enum, stream events, function/tool calls, messages, token usage, and the unified LLMResponse structure.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic-style dataclasses forming the provider-neutral wire model shared across clients and the agent loop.

nodes.name: Message
nodes.summary: Conversation message model carrying role, content, and optional tool-call/tool-result fields used throughout the agent loop and clients.

nodes.name: LLMResponse
nodes.summary: Unified non-streaming LLM response model aggregating content, tool calls, stop reason, and token usage across providers.

nodes.name: ToolCall
nodes.summary: Model for a single tool invocation request carrying the call id and the nested function-call details.

nodes.name: cli_permissions.py
nodes.summary: CLI-side permission negotiator using a termios-based interactive terminal prompt to approve/deny out-of-workspace access and record grants in the GrantStore.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses raw termios/cbreak terminal I/O via run_in_executor to read a single keypress without prompt_toolkit interference.

nodes.name: CLIPermissionNegotiator
nodes.summary: Terminal-prompt permission negotiator that asks the user to approve, approve-for-session, or reject an out-of-workspace access request and updates the GrantStore accordingly.

nodes.name: _read_with_echo
nodes.summary: Reads a single keypress from the terminal in cbreak mode with echo, restoring terminal state afterward.

nodes.name: experts.py
nodes.summary: Expert session-context models that parse host-supplied expert/team metadata, sanitize it, render system-prompt fragments, and derive skill-selection query terms.
nodes.languageNotes: Four cooperating dataclasses (profile/member/team/session) each implementing the from_meta/render_prompt/to_metadata/skill_query contract.

nodes.name: ExpertSessionContext
nodes.summary: Top-level expert context wrapping an individual profile or team, exposing from_meta parsing, render_prompt for the system prompt, to_metadata round-tripping, and a combined skill_query.

nodes.name: ExpertProfile
nodes.summary: Single-expert profile (role, expertise, instructions) with metadata parsing, prompt rendering, and skill-query term extraction.

nodes.name: ExpertTeamProfile
nodes.summary: Multi-member expert team profile aggregating ExpertTeamMember entries with combined prompt rendering and skill-query terms.

nodes.name: ExpertTeamMember
nodes.summary: Individual team-member descriptor with metadata parsing, prompt rendering, and skill-query term extraction.

nodes.name: _clean_list
nodes.summary: Normalizes a raw value into a bounded list of cleaned strings, enforcing max item and length limits.

nodes.name: memory.py
nodes.summary: Dual-file memory system: MEMORY.md core (always injected) plus topic-sharded CONTEXT.md searchable store, with the MemoryManager API, TopicStore persistence, auto-matching/recall, OpenClaw import, promotion gating, and a background MemoryExtractor.
nodes.languageNotes: 1880+ lines: ContextEntry carries HTML-comment header metadata; greedy Jaccard dedup and n-gram self-citation guards back the auto-match logic.

nodes.name: MemoryManager
nodes.summary: Primary memory API managing core/context read-write, dedup against core, search and auto-match recall, OpenClaw import, system-prompt memory block assembly, and CONTEXT-to-core promotion planning/applying.

nodes.name: TopicStore
nodes.summary: Topic-sharded persistence layer for CONTEXT entries with per-topic file storage, an index, topic matching, and merge/remove index maintenance.

nodes.name: MemoryExtractor
nodes.summary: Background extractor that runs LLM-driven memory extraction at lifecycle points and applies the resulting updates to CONTEXT.md.

nodes.name: ContextEntry
nodes.summary: Dataclass model of a single CONTEXT entry carrying content plus HTML-comment header metadata (id/created/last_used/hits/source/confidence/topic).

nodes.name: _score_memory_match
nodes.summary: Scores how well a memory line matches a query using lowercased term overlap, used by auto-match recall.

nodes.name: _parse_context_text
nodes.summary: Parses raw CONTEXT.md text into ContextEntry objects, reading per-entry HTML-comment metadata headers.

nodes.name: _is_self_citation
nodes.summary: Detects when a recalled memory is effectively a restatement of the query via n-gram overlap above a threshold, suppressing self-citation in auto-match.

nodes.name: memory_maintainer.py
nodes.summary: Startup-guarded CONTEXT.md maintainer running decay, archive cleanup, Jaccard dedup, LLM-driven compaction, and conflict resolution on a 24h interval.

nodes.name: MemoryMaintainer
nodes.summary: Interval-guarded maintainer performing CONTEXT decay (stale entries archived), archive cleanup to trash, and greedy Jaccard dedup with metadata merging.

nodes.name: MemoryMaintainer_Compact
nodes.summary: Maintainer mixin that performs LLM-driven compaction of CONTEXT entries to reduce token footprint.

nodes.name: MemoryMaintainer_Conflict
nodes.summary: Maintainer mixin that detects and resolves conflicting CONTEXT entries via an LLM pass.

nodes.name: _cluster_by_jaccard
nodes.summary: Greedily clusters CONTEXT entries by Jaccard similarity above a threshold for dedup/compaction.

nodes.name: _parse_compact_output
nodes.summary: Parses the LLM compaction output, validating against known entry ids before applying.

nodes.name: _parse_conflict_output
nodes.summary: Parses the LLM conflict-resolution output, validating against known entry ids before applying.

nodes.name: permissions.py
nodes.summary: Permission core: CapabilityPolicy, PermissionEngine (filesystem/memory/escalation checks), and GrantStore tracking prompt/session-scoped grants, plus helpers to extract absolute paths from shell commands.
nodes.languageNotes: Path-scope checks normalize and resolve filesystem paths; absolute-path extraction parses shell command bodies including quoted segments.

nodes.name: PermissionEngine
nodes.summary: Evaluates tool/filesystem/memory access against a CapabilityPolicy and GrantStore, computing escalation requests and scope-based path allowances.

nodes.name: CapabilityPolicy
nodes.summary: Immutable capability policy built from config, supporting general and filesystem-specific override derivation.

nodes.name: GrantStore
nodes.summary: Tracks granted access at prompt and session scope, including filesystem directory grants, with per-turn clearing of prompt grants.

nodes.name: PermissionDecision
nodes.summary: Result record of a permission check carrying the allow/deny outcome and any escalation request.

nodes.name: extract_absolute_paths
nodes.summary: Parses a shell command string to extract absolute filesystem paths (including quoted segments) for permission scope checking.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: ACP bridge that consumes run_agent_loop() events and translates them into ACP protocol sessionUpdate messages; hosts session lifecycle, permission negotiation, memory-proposal negotiation, session-mode classification, and the lightweight _llm/prompt extension.
nodes.languageNotes: Async ACP agent (1900+ lines): every RPC method is an async coroutine; nested negotiator classes wrap reverse-RPC calls with asyncio.wait_for timeouts.

nodes.name: BoxACPAgent
nodes.summary: Core ACP agent implementation handling initialize/newSession/prompt/cancel/extMethod RPCs, building per-mode system prompts, wiring permission and memory negotiators, and streaming run_agent_loop events as ACP session updates.

nodes.name: SessionState
nodes.summary: Per-session state container holding the Agent, output dir, permission engine/grant store, memory extractor, inject queue, session mode, and cached env/expert context.

nodes.name: _PermissionNegotiator
nodes.summary: Reverse-RPC negotiator that calls session/request_permission on the host, maps approve/approve_session/reject options to grant scopes, and records grants in the GrantStore.

nodes.name: _MemoryProposalNegotiator
nodes.summary: Reverse-RPC negotiator that surfaces CONTEXT-to-core memory promotion candidates to the host via session/memory_proposal and applies pin/skip/reject decisions.

nodes.name: run_acp_server
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the ACP stdio server: guards stdout, pins the Playwright browser path, sets up the large-buffer stdio transport, and runs the AgentSideConnection event loop.

nodes.name: _artifact_envelope
nodes.summary: Serializes an ArtifactEvent into the stable ACP artifact envelope dict (type discriminator, rel_path, sha256, mime, size) carried on tool_call_update.rawOutput.

nodes.name: _remove_inject_queue_item
nodes.summary: Drains and rebuilds the inject queue, dropping the item matching a given injection id; used to cancel a previously injected message.

nodes.name: action_hints.py
nodes.summary: Builds the ACP action-hint system-prompt fragment that nudges the model to emit settings shortcuts when memory is scarce or Playwright browser tooling is unavailable.

nodes.name: is_playwright_unavailable
nodes.summary: Inspects the MCP config file to determine whether the Playwright browser MCP server is missing or disabled.

nodes.name: build_action_hints_prompt
nodes.summary: Assembles the action-hint prompt block describing available settings shortcuts based on memory-scarce and playwright-unavailable flags.

nodes.name: debug_logger.py
nodes.summary: Structured ACP diagnostic logger writing to stderr (and an optional file), env-var controlled, ensuring stdout stays pure ACP protocol.

nodes.name: ACPDebugLogger
nodes.summary: Leveled logger (debug/info/warn/error/exception) configured from environment variables, emitting to stderr and an optional log file without touching stdout.

nodes.name: env_context.py
nodes.summary: Parses and sanitizes host-injected env_context metadata (CLI paths, platform, browser tools, runtimes) and renders it into a markdown checklist for the system prompt.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen dataclasses with strict input sanitization (unsafe-char and absolute-path guards) before trusting host-supplied metadata.

nodes.name: EnvContext
nodes.summary: Aggregate env-context model with from_meta() parsing host metadata into sanitized sub-states and is_empty() for skip detection.

nodes.name: HostRuntime
nodes.summary: Immutable record describing a host-provided runtime (name, version, path) used in the env-context checklist.

nodes.name: BrowserToolsState
nodes.summary: Immutable state describing browser-tool availability reported by the host.

nodes.name: ImageServiceState
nodes.summary: Immutable state describing image-generation service availability reported by the host.

nodes.name: build_env_context_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders an EnvContext into a markdown checklist prompt section covering CLI tools, browser tools, and image service status.

nodes.name: _sanitize_runtimes
nodes.summary: Validates and normalizes a raw runtimes list from host metadata into HostRuntime records, rejecting unsafe values.

nodes.name: _sanitize_cli
nodes.summary: Sanitizes the host-supplied CLI tool paths map, dropping entries with unsafe characters or non-absolute paths.

nodes.name: server.py
nodes.summary: Thin ACP server entry-point module that imports and invokes main() from the acp package.

nodes.name: stdio_compat.py
nodes.summary: Replicates the upstream POSIX/Windows ACP stdio helpers verbatim but raises the StreamReader buffer ceiling to 4 MiB so large inbound JSON-RPC frames don't silently kill the receive loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Custom asyncio write transport + stdin feeder; the 4 MiB _READ_LIMIT works around upstream's 64 KiB default that drops oversized frames.

nodes.name: stdio_streams_largebuf
nodes.summary: Platform-dispatching factory returning large-buffer stdin/stdout asyncio streams for the ACP transport.

nodes.name: _posix_stdio_streams
nodes.summary: Builds POSIX stdin reader (with raised limit) and stdout writer transport for the ACP stdio connection.

nodes.name: _windows_stdio_streams
nodes.summary: Builds Windows stdin reader (with raised limit) and stdout writer transport for the ACP stdio connection.

nodes.name: _StdoutTransport
nodes.summary: Minimal asyncio write transport wrapping the stdout pipe for the ACP connection.

nodes.name: jupyter_tool.py
nodes.summary: Dual-mode Jupyter sandbox execution environment: a subprocess kernel in an isolated venv for normal mode and an in-process kernel for frozen/runtime mode, exposed as the agent's code-execution tool with package auto-install and structured error codes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses asyncio.create_subprocess_exec plus run_in_executor + wait_for to keep kernel and pip operations off the event loop; IS_FROZEN flag selects between JupyterKernelSession and InProcessKernelSession.

nodes.name: SandboxEnvironment
nodes.summary: Manages an isolated venv sandbox: creates the venv, installs default and on-demand packages, verifies required modules, builds the ipykernel spec, and routes installs through a whitelist in frozen mode.

nodes.name: JupyterKernelSession
nodes.summary: Subprocess-based Jupyter kernel session that starts a kernel via a patched provisioner factory, executes code over iopub channels, and collects stdout/stderr/errors/images.

nodes.name: InProcessKernelSession
nodes.summary: In-process ipykernel session used in frozen/runtime mode, logging environment diagnostics on startup and executing code with the same stdout/stderr/error/image collection contract as the subprocess session.

nodes.name: JupyterSandboxTool
nodes.summary: Agent-facing tool that runs Python in the sandbox, managing per-session kernels, auto-installing missing modules, detecting new artifacts in the output workspace, and simplifying tracebacks for the model.

nodes.name: SandboxStatusTool
nodes.summary: Companion tool that reports sandbox readiness and active kernel session status by delegating to the JupyterSandboxTool's get_status.

nodes.name: runtime.py
nodes.summary: Resolves and provisions skill runtimes (Python sandbox + Node.js) for the agent: builds runtime context/prompts, manages downloading/extracting/verifying portable Node builds, and locates bundled runtimes in frozen mode.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes checksum-verified tar/zip extraction with path-traversal guards (_safe_extract_tar/_safe_extract_zip) and atomic manifest writes via os.replace.

nodes.name: SkillRuntime
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing a single resolved skill runtime (kind, status, provider, executable path, env vars, notes) with an availability check.

nodes.name: SkillRuntimeContext
nodes.summary: Container for the set of resolved runtimes, exposing lookup by kind and a merged environment-variable mapping across all runtimes.

nodes.name: NodeRuntimeManager
nodes.summary: Installs and discovers a managed portable Node.js runtime on macOS and Windows: downloads release archives, verifies SHA-256 checksums, safely extracts them, and persists an atomic manifest of executable paths.

nodes.name: build_skill_runtime_context
nodes.summary: Assembles a SkillRuntimeContext by resolving the Python and Node runtimes from sandbox mode, env context, and optional sandbox environment.

nodes.name: build_skill_runtime_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders a human-readable prompt fragment describing the available Python and Node runtimes for injection into the system prompt.

nodes.name: _build_python_runtime
nodes.summary: Resolves the Python skill runtime across host, frozen Windows, and sandbox-venv scenarios, returning a SkillRuntime with provider, executable path, and status.

nodes.name: _build_node_runtime
nodes.summary: Resolves the Node.js skill runtime, preferring a host runtime when present and otherwise discovering the managed portable installation.

nodes.name: _safe_manifest_path
nodes.summary: Validates and normalizes a manifest-supplied path, rejecting control characters and non-absolute paths to prevent unsafe path resolution.

nodes.name: _safe_extract_tar
nodes.summary: Extracts a tar archive while guarding against path-traversal entries that would escape the destination directory.

nodes.name: _safe_extract_zip
nodes.summary: Extracts a zip archive while rejecting entries whose resolved path would escape the destination directory.

nodes.name: _bundled_node_runtime_root
nodes.summary: Locates the bundled Node runtime root in frozen mode via an env override or the directory next to the executable, validated by manifest presence.

nodes.name: _download_url
nodes.summary: Downloads a URL to a destination file using a streamed request.

nodes.name: build_runtime.py
nodes.summary: PyInstaller build script producing the standalone box-agent runtime binary for macOS/Windows, bundling the Node runtime, portable Python/Git, and sandbox packages, then packaging the result as a tarball.
nodes.languageNotes: Platform/arch detection drives PyInstaller target args and SHA-256-verified downloads of bundled runtimes.

nodes.name: build_runtime
nodes.summary: Core build routine that runs PyInstaller, stages the bundled Node/Python/Git runtimes and sandbox packages, and assembles the final runtime directory layout.

nodes.name: detect_platform
nodes.summary: Detects the current OS and architecture and maps them to the build target identifier.

nodes.name: verify_entry_binary_arch
nodes.summary: Verifies the produced entry binary matches the expected target architecture, guarding against cross-arch build mismatches.

nodes.name: _install_portable_python_win
nodes.summary: Downloads and stages a portable Python distribution for the Windows runtime bundle.

nodes.name: _install_portable_git_win
nodes.summary: Downloads and stages a portable Git distribution for the Windows runtime bundle.

nodes.name: _install_sandbox_packages_win
nodes.summary: Installs the sandbox's default Python packages into the bundled portable Python for the Windows runtime.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses build target arguments and drives the runtime build and packaging pipeline.

nodes.name: build_win_runtime.py
nodes.summary: Windows-specific PyInstaller build script that compiles the box-agent ACP runtime, bundles the Node runtime, writes the manifest, and packages everything into a tarball.

nodes.name: _run_pyinstaller
nodes.summary: Invokes PyInstaller with the Windows-specific spec, hidden imports, and data files to produce the runtime binary.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point orchestrating the Windows runtime build: PyInstaller compile, Node install, manifest write, and tar packaging.

nodes.name: _install_node_win
nodes.summary: Installs the managed portable Node runtime into the Windows build via NodeRuntimeManager.

nodes.name: _install_to
nodes.summary: Stages built artifacts into the destination runtime directory layout for the Windows bundle.

nodes.name: tests.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs the pytest suite across a Python 3.10/3.11/3.12 matrix on every push to main and on pull requests, using uv for dependency management.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a build matrix (fail-fast: false) to run identical steps across three Python versions; checks out submodules recursively to load the skills submodule and relies on astral-sh/setup-uv with caching.

nodes.name: test
nodes.summary: Primary CI job on ubuntu-latest that checks out the repo with submodules, installs uv and the matrixed Python version, syncs all extras, and runs pytest with the network-flaky timeout test deselected.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Concise contributor reference covering repository structure, build/test commands, coding style, testing, and commit/PR conventions for the box_agent package.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Architecture and guidance document for Claude Code, detailing the box_agent execution core, LLM layer, tool system, memory, sandbox, ACP bridge, build/publish commands, and key patterns.

nodes.name: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
nodes.summary: Contributor Covenant code of conduct defining community pledge, standards, responsibilities, scope, and enforcement procedures.

nodes.name: CODE_OF_CONDUCT_CN.md
nodes.summary: Chinese translation of the Contributor Covenant code of conduct, mirroring the English pledge, standards, responsibilities, scope, and enforcement sections.

nodes.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive contributing guide covering bug reporting, feature requests, the development process, PR checklist, Python code style, type hinting, and testing requirements.

nodes.name: CONTRIBUTING_CN.md
nodes.summary: Chinese translation of the contributing guide, mirroring bug reporting, feature requests, development process, PR checklist, code style, and testing sections.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project README introducing Box-Agent's value proposition, key features (sub-agent parallelism, sandboxed execution, multi-provider LLM, context compression), installation, CLI usage, ACP integration, testing, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: README_CN.md
nodes.summary: Chinese translation of the project README, mirroring the feature overview, installation steps, CLI usage, ACP integration, testing, and FAQ sections.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python project manifest defining package metadata, runtime dependencies, console script entry points (box-agent, box-agent-acp, box-agent-build-runtime), build system, pytest, and pylint configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses setuptools build backend with project.scripts console entry points and tool.pytest asyncio_mode auto configuration.

nodes.name: analysis_prompt.md
nodes.summary: System prompt injected into the LLM for the data-analysis session mode, defining workflow priorities, visualization and Excel-export rules, sandbox document processing, and the interactive chart-data and final-report output contracts.
nodes.languageNotes: Not human documentation — this Markdown is loaded by config.py and injected verbatim as a mode-specific system prompt for data_analysis sessions.

nodes.name: config-example.yaml
nodes.summary: Example agent configuration template covering provider/api credentials, context window and retry tuning, workspace and system-prompt paths, memory and memory-extraction settings, tool toggles, and image generation, copied to config.yaml by the setup wizard.

nodes.name: mcp-example.json
nodes.summary: Example MCP server registration file defining the mcpServers map (including a disabled-by-default Playwright browser-automation server) loaded by mcp_loader.py at startup.

nodes.name: system_prompt.md
nodes.summary: Default system prompt injected into the LLM defining the agent's role, working guidelines, file/bash operation rules, the artifact output contract, safety constraints, and output expectations.
nodes.languageNotes: Not human documentation — this Markdown is loaded by config.py and injected verbatim as the agent's base system prompt.

nodes.name: ACTION_HINT_PROTOCOL.md
nodes.summary: Protocol spec for the ACP action-hint feature: defines the fenced action_hint block format, field semantics, tab whitelist, frontend streaming-parse guidance, and backend config switches so the model can surface settings shortcuts when memory is scarce or browser tools are unavailable.
nodes.languageNotes: Bilingual headings (Chinese section titles); documents a regex-based streaming fence parser contract.

nodes.name: ARTIFACT_PROTOCOL.md
nodes.summary: Specifies the unified artifact wire format emitted on ACP tool_call_update.rawOutput with a stable type discriminator, including field reference, kind-to-renderer mapping, and host implementation steps for per-session artifact tracking and file resolution.

nodes.name: CONTEXT_COMPRESSION.md
nodes.summary: In-depth design document for the two-layer context compression system (Layer 1 micro-compact + Layer 2 token-triggered summarization), covering algorithms, constants, summary markers, incomplete-message cleanup, token estimation, and provider compatibility.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest doc in the batch (407 non-empty lines) with detailed algorithm pseudo-code and a provider compatibility matrix.

nodes.name: CONTEXT_COMPRESSION_CN.md
nodes.summary: Chinese translation of the context compression design document, mirroring the two-layer compression architecture, algorithms, summary markers, and provider compatibility content.

nodes.name: DEVELOPMENT_GUIDE.md
nodes.summary: Developer onboarding guide covering project architecture, basic usage and interactive commands, integrated MCP tools, extending the agent with custom tools/MCP/skills, system prompt customization, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: DEVELOPMENT_GUIDE_CN.md
nodes.summary: Chinese translation of the development guide, mirroring architecture overview, usage, MCP/tool/skill extension steps, system prompt customization, and troubleshooting sections.

nodes.name: ENV_CONTEXT_PROTOCOL.md
nodes.summary: Protocol spec for host-injected env_context on session/new: defines field semantics (CLI paths, platform, browser_tools, memory_configured), passthrough of unknown fields, input validation rules, and how it renders as a markdown checklist in the system prompt.

nodes.name: FILESYSTEM_POLICY_PROTOCOL.md
nodes.summary: Protocol spec for host-supplied filesystem policy: defines allowed-directory injection, merge semantics, input validation, backend processing flow, interaction with the permission engine, and end-to-end negotiation examples for in/out-of-workspace writes.

nodes.name: INTEGRATION.md
nodes.summary: Index document for Box-Agent ACP integration, cataloging the protocol suite, typical host scenarios (first launch, browser request, CLI tool usage), minimal security constraints, and backend implementation locations.

nodes.name: MEMORY_INTEGRATION.md
nodes.summary: Integration guide for the dual-file memory system: configuration, the memory_write/memory_read/memory_search tool interface, CLI and ACP/runtime integration, storage layout, automatic extraction, one-time OpenClaw import, and the Python API.

nodes.name: MEMORY_MATCH_PROTOCOL.md
nodes.summary: Protocol defining what memory data is exposed to the frontend: core memory is never returned, while explicit-search and auto-matched context memory hits are surfaced with defined display boundaries.

nodes.name: MEMORY_PROPOSAL_PROTOCOL.md
nodes.summary: Protocol for the context-to-core memory promotion gate, defining three channels (push session/memory_proposal, pull list, apply), request/response payloads, decision semantics (pin/skip/reject), the ContextEntry data structure, config keys, and an integration checklist.

nodes.name: PRODUCTION_GUIDE.md
nodes.summary: Production deployment guide covering demo feature scope, upgrade directions (advanced context management, model fallback, hallucination detection), standalone runtime spawning, container deployment, resource limits, and Linux least-privilege account restrictions.

nodes.name: PRODUCTION_GUIDE_CN.md
nodes.summary: Chinese translation of the production guide, covering demo scope, upgrade directions, containerized deployment, CPU/memory/disk resource limits, and Linux account permission restrictions.

nodes.name: .gitmodules
nodes.summary: Git submodule configuration declaring the external skills repository that is loaded into the project.

nodes.name: runtime_entry.py
nodes.summary: Clean entry point for the standalone PyInstaller binary that launches the ACP server over stdio with stdout guarded for pure protocol output.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen-binary entry point; keeps stdout reserved for ACP JSON-RPC and routes diagnostics to stderr.

nodes.name: build_runtime_cli.py
nodes.summary: Console-script wrapper (box-agent-build-runtime) that dynamically loads and runs scripts/build_runtime.py to build the standalone PyInstaller artifact.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses importlib to exec the build script by file location rather than importing it as a package module.

nodes.name: architecture.html
nodes.summary: Standalone interactive HTML diagram documenting the Box-Agent architecture, including the execution core, LLM layer, tool system, and ACP bridge.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-contained HTML doc with inline styling and scripting for an interactive architecture overview.

nodes.name: host-progress-events.md
nodes.summary: Integration guide for ACP host implementers describing how to render sub-agent progress and todo-snapshot events, with a minimal TypeScript handling example.

nodes.name: README_CN.md
nodes.summary: Chinese-language guide for the examples directory, walking through the basic-tools, simple-agent, and full-agent samples with quick-start, learning paths, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: English guide for the examples directory, covering the basic-tools, simple-agent, and full-agent samples with quick-start instructions, learning paths, and troubleshooting tips.

nodes.name: MANIFEST.in
nodes.summary: Python sdist packaging manifest declaring which non-code asset files (config templates, skills, prompts) to include in the distributed package.

nodes.name: generate_skills_manifest.py
nodes.summary: Build-time script that scans bundled SKILL.md files, parses their YAML frontmatter names, and writes the builtin skills whitelist manifest used to filter orphan skills at runtime.

nodes.name: probe_relay_tool_stream.py
nodes.summary: Diagnostic probe that streams an OpenAI-compatible chat completion with tool calls to inspect how a relay provider serializes streaming tool-call deltas.

nodes.name: setup-config.ps1
nodes.summary: Windows PowerShell setup script that creates the user config directory, backs up any existing config, and downloads default config/mcp/prompt files from GitHub.
nodes.languageNotes: PowerShell with Invoke-WebRequest downloads and a Write-ColorOutput helper for colored console output.

nodes.name: setup-config.sh
nodes.summary: POSIX shell setup script that creates the user config directory, backs up any existing config, and downloads default configuration files from GitHub.
nodes.languageNotes: Linear shell script with no function definitions; mirrors the PowerShell setup flow for Unix users.

nodes.name: test_acp_streaming.py
nodes.summary: Standalone probe that spawns the ACP server as a subprocess and exercises initialize/newSession/prompt over stdio to verify real-time streaming of session-update deltas.

nodes.name: test_permission_negotiation.py
nodes.summary: Standalone probe that drives the ACP server through an out-of-workspace write to verify the in-band permission-negotiation reverse RPC under approve/reject grant modes.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the standalone runtime by running the async ACP server entry point over stdio.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Locates scripts/build_runtime.py, dynamically loads it via importlib, and invokes its main function, exiting with an error if the script is missing.

nodes.name: _parse_skill_name
nodes.summary: Reads a SKILL.md file, extracts its YAML frontmatter, and returns the declared skill name or None when missing or malformed.

nodes.name: _collect_skills
nodes.summary: Recursively walks the skills directory, parses each SKILL.md, and collects valid skill name/path entries for the manifest.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Assembles the skills manifest with the package version and timestamp and writes it as JSON to the manifest path.

nodes.name: _serialize
nodes.summary: Recursively converts arbitrary stream objects (pydantic models, dict-likes, plain objects) into JSON-serializable structures for inspection.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Loads provider config, opens a streaming OpenAI-compatible completion with a tool, and accumulates and prints the streamed tool-call deltas for diagnosis.

nodes.name: Write-ColorOutput
nodes.summary: PowerShell helper that writes a message to the console in a named color via a color-name to ConsoleColor map.

nodes.name: send
nodes.summary: Encodes a JSON-RPC object and writes it as a line to the ACP subprocess stdin, draining the stream.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Spawns the ACP server, runs the initialize/newSession/prompt handshake, and prints streamed session-update deltas with timing to verify real-time forwarding.

nodes.name: _recv_until_response
nodes.summary: Reads JSON-RPC messages from the ACP subprocess until the expected response id arrives, dispatching any intervening requests to an on_request callback.

nodes.name: run_test
nodes.summary: Sets up an isolated workspace, drives the ACP server through an out-of-workspace write, and answers the permission reverse RPC according to the grant mode to verify negotiation behavior.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses approve/reject CLI flags and runs the permission-negotiation test with the selected grant mode.

nodes.name: _playwright_env
nodes.summary: Returns an environment dict with PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH pinned to the default browsers path unless the caller already set it.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Synchronous entry point that runs the async ACP server (run_acp_server) via asyncio.run.

layers.name: Execution Core
layers.description: The agent loop heart: run_agent_loop event generator, public Agent API, event definitions, lifecycle hooks, expert sessions, retry/backoff, logging, and authentication.

layers.name: CLI & Entry Points
layers.description: Interactive prompt_toolkit CLI, in-session command handlers, setup wizard, and runnable example/probe scripts that drive the agent framework.

layers.name: ACP Protocol Bridge
layers.description: Agent Client Protocol server translating run_agent_loop events into sessionUpdate messages, plus intent classification, action hints, env context, and large-buffer stdio compatibility.

layers.name: LLM Provider Layer
layers.description: Multi-provider LLM clients (Anthropic and OpenAI-compatible) behind a unified LLMClient wrapper, with lightweight one-shot prompting, think-tag splitting, and debug logging.

layers.name: Tool System
layers.description: Built-in tool implementations (bash, file edit, todo, skill, sub-agent, vision, image-gen), the Tool/EventEmittingTool base classes, skill loader, and MCP tool integration.

layers.name: Sandbox & Runtime
layers.description: Jupyter kernel sandbox, frozen/runtime package management, and the PyInstaller standalone build pipeline plus runtime entry point and skills manifest generation.

layers.name: Memory System
layers.description: Dual-file MEMORY.md/CONTEXT.md memory engine, the background extractor/maintainer with decay/dedup/promotion, and the memory tool exposing read/write/search.

layers.name: Safety & Permissions
layers.description: Dangerous-command detection, workspace path enforcement, PPTX safety checks, and the permission-negotiation engine including the interactive CLI grant prompt.

layers.name: Configuration & Schema
layers.description: Pydantic config models and shared schema definitions, plus example config/MCP files and packaging manifests (pyproject, MANIFEST.in, gitmodules).

layers.name: Documentation & CI
layers.description: Project READMEs, contributor docs, protocol/integration specs, runtime prompt markdown, the architecture diagram, and the GitHub Actions test pipeline.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what Box-Agent is: a minimal yet professional AI agent framework with sandboxed code execution, parallel sub-agents, multi-provider LLM support, native MCP tools, and full ACP protocol support. It lays out the value proposition and feature set that the rest of this tour will trace through the actual code. Treat it as the roadmap for everything that follows.

tour.title: CLI Entry Point
tour.description: The `box-agent` console script resolves to `cli.py`, the highest fan-out module and the BFS root of the codebase. It boots the interactive prompt_toolkit session, wires up tools, config, and the setup wizard, and drives the agent. This is the front door newcomers actually run, so it shows how every other subsystem gets assembled at startup.

tour.title: Public Agent API
tour.description: `agent.py` is the public wrapper the CLI and embedders call. `Agent.run_events()` exposes the raw event stream while `Agent.run()` consumes those events and renders them to the terminal. It is the thin, stable surface that hides the complexity of the execution core behind a clean API.

tour.title: The Execution Core
tour.description: `core.py::run_agent_loop()` is the single source of truth for the agent loop, an async generator that yields structured events and performs no direct I/O. `events.py` defines the full vocabulary of those events (StepStart, ThinkingEvent, ContentEvent, ToolCallStart/Result, DoneEvent, ArtifactEvent). Together they form the event-driven contract every consumer (CLI, ACP) depends on, which is why both rank high in fan-in.
tour.languageLesson: Python async generators (`async def` + `yield`) let `run_agent_loop` stream events lazily to whatever consumes them, decoupling the agent's control flow from rendering. Consumers drive it with `async for`, and cancellation is cooperative via checked callbacks rather than hard interrupts.

tour.title: Multi-Provider LLM Layer
tour.description: The LLM layer abstracts over providers behind a single `LLMClient` wrapper. `anthropic_client.py` speaks the Anthropic protocol (including native thinking) and `openai_client.py` speaks the OpenAI protocol, both implementing a shared base so any third-party endpoint works by passing `api_base` through unchanged. This is how the core stays provider-agnostic while supporting Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepSeek, SiliconFlow, and more.

tour.title: The Tool System
tour.description: `tools/base.py` defines the abstract `Tool` contract (`to_schema`, `to_openai_schema`, `parallel_safe`) and has the highest fan-in in the project — nearly every tool extends it. `bash_tool.py` is a representative built-in showing shell execution plus background process management. This establishes the pattern by which the agent gains all of its capabilities.

tour.title: Parallel Sub-Agents
tour.description: `sub_agent_tool.py` lets the agent spawn isolated child agents that each run their own `run_agent_loop()` and return only summaries, enabling concurrent decomposition of large tasks. It builds on the EventEmittingTool pattern to stream child progress back in real time via an asyncio.Queue. This is the parallelism feature the README highlights, expressed entirely as a tool.
tour.languageLesson: Multiple `parallel_safe` tools execute concurrently via `asyncio.gather`. Sub-agents stream progress through an `asyncio.Queue` that the parent loop drains in the foreground, so child events interleave live instead of arriving only at completion.

tour.title: Sandboxed Code Execution
tour.description: `jupyter_tool.py` provides the dual-mode execution sandbox: a subprocess Jupyter kernel in an isolated venv during normal use, or an in-process kernel when running as a frozen binary. All kernel calls run in an executor with timeouts so they never block the event loop, and runtime package installs are gated by a whitelist. This is the secure code-execution engine that powers data-analysis and artifact generation.

tour.title: The Memory System
tour.description: `memory.py` implements a dual-file design: `MEMORY.md` holds always-injected core identity/preferences, while `CONTEXT.md` holds searchable, auto-extracted context retrieved on demand. A background `MemoryExtractor` writes to CONTEXT.md at key lifecycle points, with code-level dedup against core. This gives the agent durable, layered memory across sessions.

tour.title: Safety and Permissions
tour.description: `safety.py` detects dangerous commands, validates workspace paths, and auto-backs up files before modification; `permissions.py` adds in-band permission negotiation via a GrantStore and PermissionEngine consulted before each policy check. Both are high fan-in because every tool invocation flows through them. Together they enforce the guardrails that make autonomous execution safe.

tour.title: The ACP Protocol Bridge
tour.description: `acp/__init__.py` (the highest fan-out module) consumes `run_agent_loop()` events and translates them into ACP `sessionUpdate` messages, letting host applications embed the agent over JSON-RPC stdio. It forwards streaming thinking/content deltas in real time and handles reverse RPCs for permissions and memory proposals. `server.py` is the thin entry point hosts launch as `box-agent-acp`.

tour.title: Configuration
tour.description: `config.py` defines the Pydantic config models and a layered load priority (dev dir → user dir → package fallback), and `config-example.yaml` shows the user-facing settings including provider selection and `api_base`. The `provider` field decides which LLM client from Step 5 is used. This is where a newcomer wires the framework to their own API and tunes behavior.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic models validate and coerce config at load time, so malformed YAML fails fast with clear errors. The layered lookup means a dev `config.yaml` overrides the installed one, which overrides the packaged default.

tour.title: Context Compression Design
tour.description: This design document explains the two-layer context compression the README advertises: Layer 1 micro-compact replaces old tool results with placeholders every step at zero cost, and Layer 2 triggers an LLM summary once estimated tokens cross a derived threshold. It captures the rationale behind keeping long sessions within the model's context window without losing logged originals. Read it to understand why `core.py` from Step 4 manages history the way it does.

tour.title: Testing and CI
tour.description: The GitHub Actions workflow runs the pytest suite that guards the framework, serving as the capstone that shows how changes get verified before release. It ties the tour together by exercising the core loop, tools, and protocol bridges covered in earlier steps. This is the safety net that keeps the architecture you just toured working.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows use `on` triggers to run `jobs` made of sequential `steps`. Pytest's `asyncio_mode = "auto"` lets async tests run without explicit markers, matching this codebase's fully-async design.
